name: Dundonnell Wind Farm
notable: 80x Vestas V150-4.2MW. PPAs with Victorian Govt, Snowy Hydro, ALDI.

timeline.title: EES required
timeline.detail: Minister for Planning determined Environment Effects Statement required

timeline.title: EES assessment completed
timeline.detail: Minister released assessment under Environment Effects Act 1978

timeline.title: VREAS auction winner
timeline.detail: Selected as one of six winners of Victorian Renewable Energy Auction Scheme

timeline.title: Final investment decision
timeline.detail: Tilt Renewables made final investment decision on the $560M project

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

eis_specs.document_title: Dundonnell Wind Farm Environment Effects Statement (VIC DELWP EES)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Standard hub height for V150-4.2 MW on Western VIC terrain
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height; Western Victorian coastal plains, modelled from 2-year met mast and ERA5 reanalysis
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP modelling with ERA5 long-term correction; P50 net yield after wake, availability and plant performance deductions
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~18 km of new 220 kV transmission line to Mortlake substation in Western Victoria.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: New 220 kV connection to AusNet's South-West Victoria transmission system. AusNet upgraded Mortlake substation bus to accommodate Dundonnell load-flow.
eis_specs.notes: 336 MW (80 × 4.2 MW Vestas V150), Western Victoria near Mortlake. Developer: Tilt Renewables (sold share to Pacific Hydro / PowAR). VIC EES approved by Victorian Minister for Planning 2019. Now operating (2021). BOP: Zenviron. 80 turbines × 4.2 MW = 336 MW. Western VIC coastal plains — among the best onshore wind resource in Australia. 40 dBA noise limit and 2 km residential setback consistent with VIC Wind Energy Facilities guidelines.
